                  have h₁₄ : j < i := by

                    have h₁₉ : j < i := by

have h₁ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ (i j : Fin n), Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      intro i j
      classical
      rw [show (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) = (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ if h_eq : i = j then ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) else if h_lt : i < j then ω (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩) else ω (⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by
         have h : j < i := by
           cases lt_or_le i j with
           | inl h_lt => exfalso; simp_all
           | inr h_le =>
             cases lt_or_eq_of_le h_le with
             | inl h_lt => exact h_lt
             | inr h_eq => exfalso; simp_all
         exact h
      )⟩)) by
        funext ω
        rfl]
      -- The above line shows that the function is indeed of the form we want.
      -- Now, we can use the fact that the measurable functions are closed under if-then-else
      -- to conclude that the function is measurable.
      apply Measurable.ite
      ·
        -- Case: i = j
        -- We need to show that the function ω ↦ ω(⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) is measurable.
        -- Since ω ↦ ω(⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩) is a projection, it is measurable.
        apply Measurable.ite
        ·
          -- Subcase: i < j
          -- We need to show that the function ω ↦ ω(⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩) is measurable.
          -- Since ω ↦ ω(⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩) is a projection, it is measurable.
          apply measurable_pi
        ·
          -- Subcase: ¬(i < j)
          -- We need to show that the function ω ↦ ω(⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by ... )⟩) is measurable.
          -- Since ω ↦ ω(⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by ... )⟩) is a projection, it is measurable.
          apply measurable_pi
        ·
          -- The condition i < j is measurable
          exact Measurable.of_measurable_of_le (measurable_const) (measurable_const) (fun _ ↦ by simp_all)
      ·
        -- The condition i = j is measurable
        exact Measurable.of_measurable_of_eq (measurable_const) (measurable_const) (fun _ ↦ by simp_all)
      ·
        -- Case: ¬(i = j)
        -- We further split into the cases i < j and ¬(i < j)
        apply Measurable.of_measurable_of_lt (measurable_const) (measurable_const) (fun _ ↦ by
          -- Prove that the order is total
          cases le_or_lt i j <;> simp_all [lt_irrefl]
          <;> try { contradiction }
          <;> try { linarith }
          <;> try { simp_all [lt_irrefl] }
          )
    -- Now, we use the fact that the matrix is defined by its entries and that each entry is measurable.
    -- Therefore, the entire matrix is measurable.
    have h₃ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := by
      apply Matrix.measurableHom
      intro i j
      exact h₂ i j
    exact h₃
  -- Now, we can conclude that the function wignerMatrixMap n is measurable because it is a scalar multiplication of a measurable function.
  have h₄ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    have h₅ : (wignerMatrixMap n : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) = (fun ω ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • wignerMatrixMap' ω) := by
      funext ω
      rfl
    rw [h₅]
    -- Since wignerMatrixMap' is measurable and scalar multiplication is measurable, the result is measurable.
    have h₆ : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap' : WignerSpace n → Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ) := h₁
    -- Use the fact that the scalar multiplication is measurable to conclude that the composition is measurable.
    have h₇ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ (1 / Real.sqrt (n : ℝ)) • wignerMatrixMap' ω) := by
      have h₈ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) ↦ wignerMatrixMap' ω) := h₆
      -- Use the property that scalar multiplication is measurable.
      apply Continuous.measurable.comp h₈ (continuous_const.smul continuous_id)
    exact h₇
  exact h₄

have h : Measurable (wignerMatrixMap n) := by
    -- Show that the function from WignerSpace n to Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) ℝ is measurable.
    -- Use the fact that the matrix entries are measurable.
    refine' (infi measurable_const _).measurable
    universal i j
    simp only [wignerMatrixMap, wignerMatrixMap', Matrix.of_apply, wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
    split_ifs <;>
    (try { simp_all }) <;>
    (try { measurable }) <;>
    (try { -- Case: i = j
      exact Measurable.comp (measurable_pi) (measurable_fst) }) <;>
    (try { -- Case: i < j
      exact Measurable.comp (measurable_pi) (measurable_fst) }) <;>
    (try { -- Case: j < i
      exact Measurable.comp (measurable_pi) (measurable_fst) }) <;>
    (try {
      -- Use the fact that the measure is defined on the entire space
      exact Measurable.comp (measurable_pi) (measurable_fst)
    })
  exact h

have h₁ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixMap' ω) := by
    -- Use the fact that the matrix entries are measurable functions of the sample
    apply Measurable.of_bit0
    -- Prove that each entry of the matrix is measurable
    intro i j
    -- Consider the case where i = j, i < j, or j < i
    by_cases h : i = j
    · -- Case i = j
      simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixMap', h]
      <;>
      (try simp_all [Matrix.of_apply, MeasurableFunction.comp_apply, Prod.mk.injEq]) <;>
      (try measurability) <;>
      (try
        {
          refine' (measurable_fst.comp (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))).measurable
        }) <;>

        }) <;>
      (try
        {
          simp_all [Prod.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try aesop)
          <;>
          (try
            {
              exact (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))
            })
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          exact measurable_const
        })
    · -- Case i ≠ j
      by_cases h' : i < j
      · -- Subcase i < j
        simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixMap', h, h']
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Matrix.of_apply, MeasurableFunction.comp_apply, Prod.mk.injEq]) <;>
        (try measurability) <;>
        (try
          {
            refine' (measurable_fst.comp (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))).measurable
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            refine' (measurable_snd.comp (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))).measurable
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Prod.ext_iff]
            <;>
            (try aesop)
            <;>
            (try
              {
                exact (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))
              })
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            exact measurable_const
          })
      · -- Subcase j < i
        have h'' : j < i := by
          have h''' : ¬i < j := h'
          have h'''' : i ≠ j := h
          have h''''' : j < i := by
            cases' lt_or_gt_of_ne h'''' with h'''' h''''
            · exfalso
              exact h''' h''''
            · exact h''''
          exact h'''''
        simp_all [wignerMatrixEntryFunction, wignerMatrixMap', h, h']
        <;>
        (try simp_all [Matrix.of_apply, MeasurableFunction.comp_apply, Prod.mk.injEq]) <;>
        (try measurability) <;>
        (try
          {
            refine' (measurable_fst.comp (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))).measurable
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            refine' (measurable_snd.comp (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))).measurable
          }) <;>
        (try
          {
            simp_all [Prod.ext_iff]
            <;>
            (try aesop)
            <;>
            (try
              {
                exact (measurable_subtype_val : Measurable (fun (p : Subtype (fun p : Fin n × Fin n => p.1 ≤ p.2)) => p.val))
              })
          })
        <;>
        (try
          {
            exact measurable_const
          })
  -- Use the measurability of the entries to prove the measurability of the matrix
  exact h₁

have h₁ : ∀ (j : Fin n) (i : Fin n), Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
    intro j i
    have h₂ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j) := by
      -- We need to show that the function ω ↦ wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j is measurable.
      -- Since wignerMatrixEntryFunction is defined piecewise based on the comparison of i and j, we need to consider each case separately.
      -- The function is constructed using the WignerSpace ω and the indices i and j, and it either returns ω (⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩), ω (⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩), or ω (⟨(j, i), le_of_lt h_gt⟩).
      -- Each of these cases is measurable because ω is a measurable function from WignerSpace n to ℝ and the indices are fixed.
      -- The piecewise definition is based on decidable conditions (i = j, i < j, etc.), which are measurable.
      -- Therefore, the overall function is measurable as a combination of measurable functions.
      have h₃ : Measurable (fun (ω : WignerSpace n) => (if h_eq : (i : ℕ) = (j : ℕ) then ω ⟨(i, i), le_refl i⟩ else if h_lt : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) then ω ⟨(i, j), le_of_lt h_lt⟩ else ω ⟨(j, i), le_of_lt (by
        have h₄ : (j : ℕ) < (i : ℕ) := by
          by_contra h
          have h₅ : (i : ℕ) ≤ (j : ℕ) := by omega
          have h₆ : (i : ℕ) ≠ (j : ℕ) := by
            intro h₆
            simp_all
          have h₇ : (i : ℕ) < (j : ℕ) := by
            omega
          simp_all
        exact h₄)⟩)) := by
        -- We need to show that the piecewise function is measurable.
        -- Each case is measurable because it is a composition of measurable functions.
        -- The conditions (i = j, i < j, etc.) are decidable and thus measurable

rw [show (wignerMatrixMap n) = Matrix.of ∘ (fun ω => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω) by
      ext ω
      <;> simp [wignerMatrixMap', wignerMatrixEntryFunction]
      <;> rfl]
    -- The composition of measurable functions is measurable.
    apply Measurable.comp
    · -- The function `Matrix.of` is measurable.
      exact inferInstance
    · -- The function `fun ω => wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω` is measurable.
      -- This is because each entry of `wignerMatrixEntryFunction ω i j` is measurable in `ω`,
      -- and the product of measurable functions is measurable.
      exact inferInstance
